header.label: Teacher Retirement Analytics
header.headline: 8.6 L teachers retiring in ~5 years — Foundational & Prep hardest hit · 2023-24
header.sub: 98.1 L total teachers. Retirement wave (57+): 8.6 L (9%). Over 65: 27T (0.3%). Regular: 84%, Contract: 16%.

cards.label: Retirement Wave (5yr)
cards.note: 9% of the teaching workforce is aged 57 or above. Regular teachers: 10% retiring, Contract: 5% retiring.

cards.label: Over-65 Teaching
cards.note: 0.3% of teachers are over 65 and still in service. Government schools: 0.1%, Private Unaided: 0.6%.

cards.label: Prime Working Age
cards.barLabel: 77.5 L teachers in prime age band
cards.note: 79% of teachers are under 52 — the workforce has a healthy age base. 12% are in the 52–56 senior-but-not-retiring band.

cards.label: Appointment Type Split
cards.note: Regular: 82.8 L (84%), Contract: 15.3 L (16%). Regular teachers have 10% retiring vs 5% for contract.

2023-24.ageBandsSource: Over 9% of the teaching force is aged 57 or above and approaching retirement — concentrated in government schools where hiring pipelines need activation now to avoid gaps.
2023-24.ageBandsNote: 79% of teachers are under 52 — the workforce has a healthy age base. The 57–65+ cohort (9%) represents a significant near-term exit.

catRetireRows.name: Foundational & Prep
catRetireRows.note: 9% of Foundational & Prep teachers in retirement wave (age 57+)

catRetireRows.name: Middle School
catRetireRows.note: 9% of Middle School teachers in retirement wave (age 57+)

catRetireRows.name: Secondary
catRetireRows.note: 9% of Secondary teachers in retirement wave (age 57+)

2023-24.catRetireSource: Foundational & Prep schools face the most acute retirement wave — experienced teachers at this level are hardest to replace given subject-specialist requirements.
2023-24.catRetireNote: Foundational & Prep schools have the highest near-term retirement share at 9%. Combined with stressed PTR, this creates a compound risk.

mgmtRetireRows.name: Central Govt
mgmtRetireRows.note: 10% of Central Govt teachers approaching retirement

mgmtRetireRows.name: State Govt
mgmtRetireRows.note: 11% of State Govt teachers approaching retirement

mgmtRetireRows.name: Govt Aided
mgmtRetireRows.note: 12% of Govt Aided teachers approaching retirement

mgmtRetireRows.name: Private Unaided
mgmtRetireRows.note: 5% of Private Unaided teachers approaching retirement

2023-24.mgmtRetireSource: Government-managed schools carry the largest share of near-retirement teachers — strategic pre-emptive recruitment is essential to maintain continuity of instruction.
2023-24.mgmtRetireNote: Govt Aided schools have the highest retirement concentration at 12%. Government schools carry the largest absolute burden.

insight1.label: Teachers Retiring in ~5 Years · 2023-24
insight1.note: 8.6 L teachers aged 57 or above. This 9% workforce exit requires urgent succession planning across all school categories and management types.
insight1.delta_label: Share of total teacher workforce retiring within 5 years

insight2.label: Teachers Above Retirement Age Still Serving · 2023-24
insight2.note: 27T teachers (0.3%) are over 65 and still in service — concentrated in private and aided schools, often on contract appointments beyond standard retirement age.

header.label: Teacher Retirement Analytics
header.headline: 9.3 L teachers retiring in ~5 years — Foundational & Prep hardest hit · 2024-25
header.sub: 1 Cr total teachers. Retirement wave (57+): 9.3 L (9%). Over 65: 32T (0.3%). Regular: 84%, Contract: 16%.

cards.label: Retirement Wave (5yr)
cards.note: 9% of the teaching workforce is aged 57 or above. Regular teachers: 10% retiring, Contract: 5% retiring.

cards.label: Prime Working Age

cards.note: 79% of teachers are under 52 — the workforce has a healthy age base. 12% are in the 52–56 senior-but-not-retiring band.

cards.label: Appointment Type Split
cards.note: Regular: 84.7 L (84%), Contract: 16.5 L (16%). Regular teachers have 10% retiring vs 5% for contract.

2024-25.ageBandsSource: Over 9% of the teaching force is aged 57 or above and approaching retirement — concentrated in government schools where hiring pipelines need activation now to avoid gaps.
